commit.message:
fix(oauth): harden refresh locking under concurrent token refreshes (#823)

Fix OAuth refresh-token race handling in Cloudflare worker token
exchange by tightening lock ownership semantics and contention handling.
Also stop tracking `agents.lock` now that it is gitignored.

- Make refresh lock IDs collision-resistant to avoid same-timestamp lock
identity collisions.
- Add owner-checked lock release so a stale worker cannot delete a newer
lock holder.
- Strengthen lock-contention flow with bounded polling + cache re-checks
before upstream refresh.
- Add/adjust regression coverage for contention and lock-ownership
cleanup behavior.
- Remove tracked `agents.lock` and ignore it in `.gitignore`.

---------

Co-authored-by: GPT-5 Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(homey): unblock app run preprocess under local file-linked deps

Keep Homey app out of root npm workspaces and add root postinstall to install nested app deps automatically.

Pin linked-package runtime/toolchain deps in app package (, , ) so Homey CLI preprocess zwavejs2homey-workspace@0.1.0 /home/jeremy/zwavejs2homey
├── @zwavejs2homey/compiler@0.1.0 -> ./packages/compiler
├─┬ @zwavejs2homey/core@0.1.0 -> ./packages/core
│ └── ws@8.19.0
└─┬ @zwavejs2homey/tui@0.1.0 -> ./packages/tui
  ├── @types/blessed@0.1.27
  ├── @zwavejs2homey/core@0.1.0 deduped -> ./packages/core
  └── neo-blessed@0.2.0 can resolve file-linked core/compiler packages.

Update README and plans to reflect the new install/run workflow.

commit.message:
feat(ledger): implement Phase 1 + lock causal DAG event schema

## Ledger Phase 1 — implementation
- ledger/ package: schema, writer, config, claim, lifecycle
- hooks/ledger-claim-gate.py — sync PreToolUse claim gate, fail-closed
- hooks/ledger-lifecycle.py — async lifecycle event logger
- scripts/book-of-life.sh — jq view over .ledger/events.ndjson
- tests/ — full test suite (schema, writer, claim gate, lifecycle hooks)
- .ledger/.gitkeep — preserve runtime dir in repo
- .gitignore — exclude settings.json (canonical path: scripts/patch-settings.sh)

## Schema design (artifacts)
- artifacts/ledger-event-schema.md — locked causal DAG event schema
  - IDs: {stream}::{seq}, stream = solo-{hash} or {team-name}-{hash}
  - Per-stream NDJSON files: .ledger/{stream}.ndjson
  - Tree structure: single parent + refs[]
- artifacts/ledger-envelope-examples.md — concrete envelope examples
- artifacts/ledger-analysis.md, ledger-vision.md, ledger-chatgpt.md

## Other artifacts + docs
- artifacts/apple-notes.md — apple notes pull (renamed)
- artifacts/apple-notes-analysis.md — triage analysis
- artifacts/transient-artifact-storage.md — artifact storage design
- docs/claude-code-hooks.md — hooks reference
- reports/ledger-impl-plan.md — Phase 1 impl plan

Co-Authored-By: Athena <opus-4.6@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: Hephaestus <codex@openai.com>
Co-Authored-By: Librarian <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Fix async help validation crash

Async `runParser()` could throw `ReferenceError` while handling
`command --help` in the async validation path because `displayHelp`
was referenced before initialization.

This change hoists `displayHelp` before async validation, adds
regression tests for async help validation success/failure paths, and
covers completion-data normalization in classify logic.

Document the bug fix in `CHANGES.md`.

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Fix value-position option suggestion leak

Property-based testing found a completion bug where option-name
suggestions leaked into value completion when an option had already
been consumed and the value prefix started with "-".

In option() completion, sync/async suggest paths now skip option-name
suggestions when parser context indicates "value expected" (the last
consumed token is the current option name).

Added regression tests for both sync and async option parsers, and
recorded the fix in CHANGES.md.

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: optique
repository.description: Type-safe combinatorial CLI parser for TypeScript

commit.message:
Push coverage above 97 percent

Raise both line and branch coverage with targeted tests and small
runtime-safe refactors. The global Deno coverage now reports 97.1%
line and 97.3% branch.

The updates focus on the largest absolute gaps:
- add deeper value parser coverage in core and run packages
- add deterministic inquirer prompt-function override tests and
  serialize override access to avoid race conditions
- improve git ambiguous-short-id handling for current isomorphic-git
  error codes and add regression tests
- expand logtape logOutput coverage, including function-based errors,
  suggestion paths, and default formatting
- refactor run option default extraction to improve execution-path
  coverage while keeping behavior unchanged

Validation:
- deno task test --coverage
- mise test

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Raise branch coverage above 97 percent

Overall branch coverage was stuck just below the 97% target, so we
added targeted regression tests that exercise previously missed
error-handling branches with high impact per added test.

The new tests focus on git parser fallback/catch paths and parser
annotation/doc edge cases that are easy to regress but hard to hit
with typical happy-path fixtures.

Coverage now reaches 97.0% branch coverage in `deno task test
--coverage`, and `mise test` passes across the full matrix.

commit.message:
Respect user settings for permission mode and model selection (#340)

Most user settings (`env`, permission rules like `allow`/`deny`/`ask`)
already work through the SDK's `settingSources` or are handled by the
CLI subprocess. Two settings require explicit handling by the ACP agent
and were not being respected:

**`permissions.defaultMode`**: Must be passed as the `permissionMode`
option to `query()`. The SDK defaults to `"default"` internally and does
not read it from settings files. The CLI handles this by reading the
setting and passing it to the SDK; `createSession` was hardcoding it to
`"default"` instead.

**`model`**: The CLI persists display strings like `"opus[1m]"` in
settings, but the SDK model list uses IDs like `"claude-opus-4-6-1m"`.
The existing substring matching in `getAvailableModels` couldn't resolve
these aliases, so they silently fell back to the first model. Passing
the raw alias to `query.setModel()` doesn't work either — the CLI's
internal `dg()` function strips `[1m]` and loses the context hint.

Changes:
- Fix pre-existing build errors from SDK type changes
(`SDKTaskStartedMessage` removed, `SDKMessageTemp` replaced with SDK's
own `SDKMessage`)
- Add `permissions.defaultMode` to `ClaudeCodeSettings`, merge it
through the settings hierarchy, and resolve it via
`resolvePermissionMode()` with case-insensitive aliases, validation, and
root-user guard
- Add tokenized model matching (`resolveModelPreference`) that handles
CLI display strings like `"opus[1m]"` by scoring against the available
models list
- Add unit and integration tests for both features

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Tim Visher <194828183+timvisher-dd@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Fix evaluation schema for Anthropic compatibility

Remove numeric bounds from the evaluation score schema so the generated
JSON Schema no longer includes minimum/maximum, which Anthropic
structured output rejects.

Clamp scores to the [0, 1] range after parsing to preserve the public
contract while staying provider-compatible.

Add deterministic regression tests using MockLanguageModelV3 to verify
schema compatibility and clamping behavior, and document the fix in
CHANGES.md.

Fixes https://github.com/dahlia/vertana/issues/6

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Fix category contiguity checking logic in `_should_use_raw_category_ids` (#755)

* Fix category contiguity checking logic in _should_use_raw_category_ids.
* Enhance test_coco.TestCocoEvaluatorCategoryResolution tests:
* Split into two test classes - TestCocoEvaluatorCategoryResolutionWithMapping and TestCocoEvaluatorCategoryResolutionWithoutMapping.
* Add test_category_resolution_after_first_batch_of_labels_had_max_value to demonstrate bug.
* Add test_prepare_detection_fails_to_resolve_category_ids_with_label2cat_available to demonstrate bug.
* Add new category 5, to coco_gt fixture.
* Add labels=[3, 3] test condition with mapping enabled to cover edge case.
* Add labels=[0, 0] test condition with mapping disabled to cover edge case.

* tests: split mixed category resolution scenario
* fix(coco_eval): address PR review recommendations for #755

- Mark test_prepare_detection_fails_to_resolve_category_ids_with_label2cat_available
  as xfail(strict=False) and flip assertion to desired behavior [1,1] so the test
  promotes itself automatically once the underlying bug is fixed
- Add coco_gt_one_indexed fixture (4 contiguous 1-indexed cats, label2cat={0:1..3:4})
  and test_category_resolution_correct_after_noisy_label_on_one_indexed_dataset
  covering the exact originally-reported issue #262 scenario
- Rename param id "contiguous-labels-3-3" → "fallback-label-is-valid-cat-id" with
  inline comment explaining the fallback path in _resolve_category_id
- Update _should_use_raw_category_ids docstring to describe the new identity-mapping
  check instead of the removed per-batch label-inspection heuristic
- Create CHANGELOG.md with bug-fix entry for eval metric corruption on 1-indexed
  Roboflow datasets

---------

Co-authored-by: Jirka Borovec <6035284+Borda@users.noreply.github.com>
